message:
feat(extensions_js): Node.js builtin compatibility overlay system and expanded builtin shims

Add a dynamic builtin compatibility overlay module system that generates
per-extension synthetic ESM wrappers for Node.js builtin imports. When an
extension uses named imports from builtins (e.g. `import { createServer }
from "node:http2"`), the resolver now detects these at module resolution
time, generates a compatibility overlay that re-exports the namespace
members as proper named exports, and redirects the import to the overlay.
This fixes extensions that rely on named destructuring from Node builtins
which QuickJS/rquickjs previously could not resolve.

Key components of the overlay system:
- extract_builtin_import_names(): scans extension source for named import
  bindings from builtin specifiers across all alias variants
- generate_builtin_compat_overlay_module(): emits synthetic ESM that
  re-exports namespace members with fallback to default export properties
- builtin_overlay_module_key(): SHA-256-based cache keys for overlays
- maybe_register_builtin_compat_overlay(): stateful registration with
  invalidation when import sets change
- Integration in PiJsResolver::resolve() gated on compat scan mode

Expand canonical_node_builtin() with 8 new builtins: http2, stream/web,
constants, tls, tty, zlib, perf_hooks, vm, v8, worker_threads — covering
the remaining Node.js core modules referenced by the extension corpus.

Add extract_static_require_specifiers(): a lexical scanner for CJS
require() calls that correctly skips comments, string literals, and
template literals to avoid false-positive matches from code generation
strings. This enables accurate dependency analysis for extensions using
CommonJS patterns.

Improve import_names_regex to handle mixed default+named import syntax
(e.g. `import defaultExport, { namedExport } from "pkg"`). Factor out
is_global_compat_scan_mode() for process-level compat detection, and
extend stub resolution in Pattern 4 to also fire in compat scan mode
so corpus runs can proceed past optional package holes deterministically.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
